summary: Bulk merge PR touching two small decompilation cleanup/matching areas: simplified redundant temporaries in Princess Peach's Castle stage code and replaced a MWERKS_GEKKO inline assembly implementation of Ness Yo-Yo event logic with C that preserves the needed codegen side effect through a volatile owner-pointer comparison. No PR body or review discussion was available, so intent is inferred from the diff.

what_changed.details: In grCastle_801CDFD8, removed redundant local variables such as params, params2, neg_one, zero, base_value, and final_value. The function now reads grCs_804D6970->xA into a single range variable, calls HSD_Randi(range) only when nonzero, and assigns castle9 fields directly.

Removed the MWERKS_GEKKO inline assembly version of it_2725_Logic59_EvtUnk and the NOT_IMPLEMENTED fallback. Added a C implementation that gets the Item with GET_ITEM, calls it_8026B894(gobj, ref_gobj), then performs a volatile read/comparison involving ip->xDD4_itemVar.nessyoyo.x10 and ref_gobj.

smart_moves: Reduced grCastle_801CDFD8 to direct field assignments while preserving the observed behavior: set castle9.xDE_b0 true, derive a random offset from grCs_804D6970->xA when nonzero, assign xD4 to grCs_804D6970->x8 plus the random result, reset xDC/xDA/xD8 to -1, and set xD6 to 0.
Kept a single range local in grCastle_801CDFD8 rather than repeatedly loading or naming every intermediate compiler artifact, improving readability without changing the random-range guard.

Converted it_2725_Logic59_EvtUnk from inline assembly to C while retaining the otherwise-unused post-call load/comparison through a volatile pointer expression, likely to reproduce required code generation.
decomp_lessons: When old decomp output contains many artificial temporaries, collapse them into direct struct field accesses and literal assignments if the resulting C still matches and remains clear.
A small inline asm function can sometimes be replaced by C plus a deliberate volatile read to force an otherwise dead load or comparison to remain in generated code.
For matching, unused comparisons may need to be expressed as no-op ternary or cast-to-void expressions when they correspond to preserved machine instructions.
Guard HSD_Randi calls with the same zero-range behavior as the original code; here a zero range explicitly yields rand_result = 0.
naming_conventions: Use concise local names for meaningful values, e.g. random_range was shortened to range in grCastle_801CDFD8.
Preserve established project accessor macros and typedefs such as GET_GROUND, GET_ITEM, Ground_GObj, Item_GObj, Item, and HSD_GObj.
Existing generated or inferred field names were retained, including grCs_804D6970->xA, grCs_804D6970->x8, gp->gv.castle9.xDE_b0, xD4, xDC, xDA, xD8, xD6, and ip->xDD4_itemVar.nessyoyo.x10.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed a MWERKS_GEKKO asm block for it_2725_Logic59_EvtUnk and replaced it with matching C.
Used `HSD_GObj* volatile* owner_ptr = &ip->xDD4_itemVar.nessyoyo.x10;` to force a load from the Ness Yo-Yo owner/reference field.
Used `(void) ((*owner_ptr == ref_gobj) ? 0 : 0);` as an intentional no-op comparison, likely to keep the compare/load sequence present even though the boolean result is unused.
Eliminated artificial locals in grCastle_801CDFD8, relying on direct assignments to match rather than overfitting to decompiler-style temporaries.
follow_up_queries: Find related PRs merged into pr/bulk around 2026-05-27 for original context behind the grCastle_801CDFD8 cleanup.
Search for other inline asm removals using volatile pointer no-op comparisons to preserve matching loads.
Check whether it_2725_Logic59_EvtUnk is now fully matching across supported compilers after replacing MWERKS_GEKKO asm.
